description_long: This is a compact, single-story house with a rectangular footprint and a gable roof covered in greenish shingles. The front facade features a small covered entryway with a peaked roof, flanked by two windows. The house has two prominent chimneys, each with a pair of terracotta chimney pots, rising from the roof ridge. The exterior walls are finished in a rough, grayish-brown stucco or concrete texture. The rear of the house has two additional windows, while one side features a plain door and the other side is mostly blank except for the entryway extension. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of early to mid-20th-century suburban or rural homes.
description: A small, single-story house with a green gable roof, two chimneys, and a rough gray exterior. The front features a covered entryway and two windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a peaked roof, two windows, and both chimneys visible.
description_view_1: Side view with the entryway extension visible, showing the gable end and both chimneys.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying two windows and the back slope of the roof, with both chimneys visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain door and the gable end, entryway extension partially visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Central front door with a small peaked roof overhang and no steps, flanked by two windows.
